title: Job AI Hub
description: AI-augmented job hunting command center — pipeline, documents, and interview prep in one workspace.
subtitle: The command center for your job hunt, supercharged
overview: A full-stack personal workspace for active job seekers. Track applications in a Kanban pipeline, tailor resumes and cover letters with context-aware AI, prep for interviews with question banks and mock sessions, and manage everything from a single profile-linked ecosystem — with bring-your-own-key support for OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google.

coreConcept.summary: One connected system for pipeline tracking, AI document workflows, and interview preparation.
bullets: Application-centric workspace — each role gets cover letters, interviews, Q&A, resumes, and notes
AI that reads your profile, scraped job posts, and company research — not generic one-off prompts
Action-taking chat assistant with 40+ tools to create and update records across the app
Job post inbox with URL scraping before committing to an application
Resume analyzer, structured resume editor, and cover letter generation with version history
Interview sets, mock sessions, rubric-based AI feedback, and code questions with test execution
Full JSON export/import for data portability

features: Dashboard: pipeline stats, recent applications, weekly activity, resume health, and quick actions
Applications: Kanban pipeline (Saved → Applied → Interviewing → Offer → Rejected → Withdrawn)
Per-application tabs: Cover Letter, Interviews, Q&A, Resume, Notes
Job posts: scrape listings from URLs, review extracted details, convert to applications
Companies: AI-enriched dossiers (culture, industry, interview focus)
Resumes: structured editor, PDF analysis, translation copies, template packs
Cover letters: AI generation linked to application and job profile context
Interviews: question banks, multi-round sets, mock runs, and automated feedback
AI chat: conversational assistant that creates applications, letters, and interview plans
Profile import: extract structured data from resume PDFs
BYOK: user-supplied API keys — no bundled model markup
Marketing site with invite-only MVP access flow

frontendStack: Next.js 16 (App Router) with React 19 and TypeScript 5.7
Tailwind CSS with Radix UI / shadcn-style components
Monaco Editor for code question practice
Vercel AI SDK with OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google providers
projectStructure: Next.js monolith: App Router pages + /app/api/* route handlers
Supabase: Postgres, Auth (SSR cookies), and Storage
AI layer: tool-calling chat, structured output contracts, and context-aware generation
Job post scraping and company research pipelines
Resume/cover letter document workflows with PDF generation (pdf-lib, html2pdf)
Interview engine with Piston and Playwright code execution runners
Rate limiting via Upstash Redis; monitoring via Sentry
Testing: Vitest (unit/integration/component) + Playwright e2e + k6 stress tests
GitLab CI with automated fix/retry orchestration
deployment: Supabase cloud for database, auth, and file storage
Environment-driven marketing config (contact email, demo video URL)
Sentry for error tracking and performance monitoring
Local dev with Supabase CLI and Docker

personalExperience:
Job AI Hub started as a v0 prototype and grew into a product I actually wanted to use during my own job search. The hardest part wasn't any single feature — it was keeping profile, company, job post, application, and artifact data connected so AI generation always had the right context.

Building the action-taking chat (40+ tools), the application workspace with five linked tabs, and the interview code execution pipeline pushed me across the full stack: Supabase tenancy, structured AI outputs, PDF/resume parsing, and a testing setup that spans unit tests through Playwright e2e and k6 load tests.

I'm treating it as an MVP with invite-only access while I iterate on the marketing site and core workflows. The BYOK model was a deliberate choice — I wanted powerful AI without locking users into bundled pricing.
